data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): CLEAN, no findings.

T8 is an overlay that fires only when a plan DEFINES an LLM/agent system (prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, output schemas, enums). This plan defines an attestation/signing mechanism — a deterministic cryptographic concern (HMAC-sign the verdict, bind to the latest material-event commit, invalidate on material edit, record coverage). It authors no new prompt, sub-agent, decision protocol, counter, or output enum, so the overlay does not apply.

Tool-grounded confirmation that the vocabulary the plan references is already fully specified (no undefined-but-referenced schema/enum/protocol):
- "material" edit set (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition; not tags/comments/links/assignee) is concretely realized: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:7-9 and material_fingerprint via current_material_fingerprint() (attest.py:127-154).
- "reuse the close-gate signing machinery / HMAC / no new key custody" resolves to src/rebar/signing.py (HMAC-SHA256, ALGORITHM at :50) and attest.py:3-10 (sign_plan_review reuses signing.sign_manifest).
- "coverage recorded" maps to an existing, defined field: plan_review_verdict.schema.json:46-50 (coverage object) and the counts the manifest signs (attest.py:32, 39-40).
- "material_fingerprint bound into the signed attestation" is already a defined schema field: plan_review_verdict.schema.json:51-54.

No T8 structural gaps proven; emitting no findings for this criterion is the correct outcome.
